content:
Hi everyone,

First message from me. Since I've already submitted a small amount of patches and plan to stick around, I thought it might be useful to join the Zulip community :)

I want to implement a Rust abstraction for streaming DMA mappings (GitHub issue #1020). The DMA abstraction currently covers coherent allocation only; dma_map_single, the dma_sync_* family and dma_mapping_error have no Rust side yet.

I searched the lists and the issue tracker and did not find prior work or a claim on it, so I wanted to flag it here before starting. I will sketch something out on my own first and come back with questions once concrete ones come up

Best,

Maurice

Hi Maurice,

Welcome to the community!

Do you have a specific use-case for this? For code to go upstream we typically need a user. In this case we well sooner than later need this, so it makes absolutely sense to work on this if you have the cycles. But if you do not have an immediate use-case, expect the patches to stay on the list for a bit before we have a user that allows us to merge them.

I don't have a specific use-case for this yet, except of adding it to the PCI EDU sample driver, once it is upstream. Not sure if this counts as valid use-case.

Anyways, I will work on this. Doesn't matter if it stays a while on the list.
